intent: We are not building the Forge Agent architecture. We are building a Mac Consensus Dev Agent that will use consensus AI to develop high quality software. Use all the documents in the local repo to determine the architecture. You should apply Max impl_files per PR: 8–10. Beyond that, split into multiple PRs. Max acceptance criteria: 8–10. More than that is a scope problem.
scope_statement:
Build the complete Crafted Dev Agent — a native macOS two-process AI coding agent consisting of a Swift shell (UI, Touch ID, Keychain, XPC) and a Python backend (consensus engine, build pipeline, GitHub integration, document store, multi-agent coordination, holistic review, TRD workflow, recovery/state management, CI pipeline, cross-run learning). The system is specified across 16 TRDs (TRD-1 through TRD-17) and must be decomposed into an ordered PRD plan respecting inter-TRD dependencies, with each PRD producing 8-10 implementation files per PR and 8-10 acceptance criteria per PR. Operator gap answers confirm that TRD-18 (Crafted Connect SDK), generate_single() on ConsensusEngine, existing test coverage, and cross-TRD build ordering should all be resolved during the PRD decomposition process using best practices and the Depends-on/Required-by fields in each TRD header.

Ambiguity clarifications:
  - This needs to be resolved using the PRD process using best practices.
  - This needs to be resolved using the PRD process using best practices.
  - TRD-18 was combined with TRD-17
  - build the complete system

Operator on 'Is this a greenfield build of the complete Crafted Dev Agent':
greenfield build. We have some code that manages automation and agent operations, that will be shared later. The intent is to build clean code though.

Operator on 'Should the PRD plan include the Swift shell (TRD-1, TRD-8) a':
it should include both so that a holistic product can be built

Operator on 'For the TRD-18 Crafted Connect SDK gap: should a lightweight':
TRD-18 was combined with TRD-17 and resolved using the PRD process using best practices.

Operator on 'The 35 regression tests in test_regression_taxonomy.py (TRD-':
This needs to be resolved using the PRD process using best practices.

prd_plan.title: Repository Skeleton, Forge Standards, and Project Scaffold
Security Foundations — Path Validation, Branch Sanitization, and API Error Classification
Configuration, Audit Infrastructure, and Shared Contracts
LLM Provider Adapters — Claude and OpenAI with Retry and Telemetry
Consensus Engine — Parallel Generation, Comparative Evaluation, and Single-Provider API
GitHub Integration Layer — GitHubTool Core Operations
Document Store — Parsing, Chunking, Embedding, and Injection Defense
Thread State Store, Checkpoint Persistence, and Recovery Foundation
PRD Planner and PR Planner — Decomposition Engine
CI Workflow Generation, CI Checker, and Workflow Hardening
Lint Gate and Self-Correction Loop
Failure Handler — Strategy Dispatch, Fix Scoring, and Classification
Context Manager and Repository Context Fetcher
Build Memory and Build Rules Engine — Cross-Run Learning
Build Pipeline Orchestration — BuildDirector Core
Multi-Agent Coordination — Build Ledger
PR Review Ingester, Branch Scaffold, and Build Interface Map
Repository Bootstrap and Notifier
Python Backend Entry Point, Startup Sequence, and Command Router
Holistic Code Review — ReviewDirector and Five Lenses
TRD Development Workflow — TRDSession and Adaptive Language Layer
Failure Taxonomy Documentation and Regression Test Suite (v39 Contract)
Swift Shell — KeychainKit Module
Swift Shell — AuthKit Module and Session State Machine
Swift Shell — XPCBridge Module and Wire Protocol
Swift Shell — ProcessManager Module and Backend Lifecycle
Swift Shell — Settings Module, Onboarding Flow, and Schema Migration
Swift Shell — Document Import Module
Swift Shell — AppShell, Three-Panel Layout, and Root View Hierarchy
Swift Shell — Logging, Localization, and Auto-Update Infrastructure
Mac CI Runner Workflow — crafted-ci-macos.yml
Self-Healing Observability Loop — Detection and Classification
Self-Healing Remediation Engine
Swift Shell — Health Dashboard, Issue Reporting, and Diagnosis UI
Integration Testing and Smoke Test Suite
XPC Integration Test and End-to-End Startup Validation
Figma Design Pipeline Integration
CONSULTANT Mode UI and Client Summary Pipeline
Operational Runbook, Security Checklist, and Final Build Validation

1.title: Add repository root metadata files
1.summary: Establishes the repository root with VERSION file (38.153.0), LICENSE, and .gitignore (Python __pycache__, .egg-info, build/, dist/, .venv, Xcode DerivedData, .DS_Store, *.xcuserstate). No code, no config — just root-level non-code assets and directory placeholders.

2.title: Add forge-standards documents (ARCHITECTURE, INTERFACES, DECISIONS, CONVENTIONS)
2.summary: Creates the four canonical forge-standards markdown documents: ARCHITECTURE.md (two-process architecture overview, Python backend + Swift shell, XPC bridge), INTERFACES.md (placeholder interface contracts between subsystems), DECISIONS.md (ADR log initialized with decision to use two-process architecture), CONVENTIONS.md (naming, file layout, import, and commit conventions). All prose/markdown only.

3.title: Add Python packaging configuration (pyproject.toml, requirements.txt, conftest.py)
3.summary: Creates the Python backend config: pyproject.toml (PEP 621, project name crafted-dev-agent, version 38.153.0, requires-python >=3.12, setuptools build-system), requirements.txt with all TRD-specified dependencies (anthropic, openai, PyGithub, faiss-cpu, sentence-transformers, python-docx, pdfminer.six, pyyaml, ruff, pytest, numpy), and root conftest.py for pytest discovery. No src/ packages yet.

4.title: Create src/ Python package directory tree — batch 1 (part 1/2)
4.summary: Creates the first batch of src/ directories with empty __init__.py files so backend subpackages are importable. Directories: src/, src/consensus/, src/build_pipeline/, src/github_integration/, src/document_store/, src/multi_agent/, src/holistic_review/, src/trd_workflow/, src/recovery/.

5.title: Create src/ Python package directory tree — batch 1 (part 2/2)
5.summary: Creates the first batch of src/ directories with empty __init__.py files so backend subpackages are importable. Directories: src/, src/consensus/, src/build_pipeline/, src/github_integration/, src/document_store/, src/multi_agent/, src/holistic_review/, src/trd_workflow/, src/recovery/.

6.title: Create src/ Python package directory tree — batch 2
6.summary: Extends the src/ tree with remaining backend subpackages: src/ci_pipeline/, src/cross_run_learning/, src/xpc_bridge/, src/settings/, src/state_management/. Each gets an empty __init__.py. Combined with PR-4, this completes the full Python package hierarchy matching all TRDs.

7.title: Create tests/ directory scaffold — batch 1 (part 1/2)
7.summary: Creates the first batch of the tests/ directory tree mirroring src/ subpackages. Each test subdirectory gets an __init__.py and a placeholder test file containing a single passing test. Directories: tests/, tests/consensus/, tests/build_pipeline/, tests/github_integration/, tests/document_store/.

8.title: Create tests/ directory scaffold — batch 1 (part 2/2)
8.summary: Creates the first batch of the tests/ directory tree mirroring src/ subpackages. Each test subdirectory gets an __init__.py and a placeholder test file containing a single passing test. Directories: tests/, tests/consensus/, tests/build_pipeline/, tests/github_integration/, tests/document_store/.

9.title: Create tests/ directory scaffold — batch 2
9.summary: Second batch of tests/ scaffold: tests/multi_agent/, tests/holistic_review/, tests/trd_workflow/, tests/recovery/. Each gets __init__.py and a placeholder passing test.

10.title: Create tests/ directory scaffold — batch 3
10.summary: Final batch of test scaffold directories: tests/ci_pipeline/, tests/cross_run_learning/, tests/xpc_bridge/, tests/settings/, tests/state_management/. Each gets __init__.py and a placeholder passing test. After this PR, every src/ subpackage has a corresponding tests/ subpackage.

11.title: Add state_management tests/ placeholder
11.summary: Adds the final tests/state_management/ directory with __init__.py and placeholder test, completing 1:1 parity between src/ and tests/ directories.

12.title: Create Swift Xcode project scaffold with Package.swift and module stubs (part 1/2)
12.summary: Creates the Crafted/ directory with Package.swift defining all Swift module targets (AppShell, AuthKit, KeychainKit, XPCBridge, ProcessManager, BuildStream, Settings, DocImport) as library targets. Each module gets a directory under Crafted/Sources/ with a single placeholder .swift file containing an empty enum namespace marker. Package.swift declares swift-tools-version 5.9, platform macOS 14.

13.title: Create Swift Xcode project scaffold with Package.swift and module stubs (part 2/2)
13.summary: Creates the Crafted/ directory with Package.swift defining all Swift module targets (AppShell, AuthKit, KeychainKit, XPCBridge, ProcessManager, BuildStream, Settings, DocImport) as library targets. Each module gets a directory under Crafted/Sources/ with a single placeholder .swift file containing an empty enum namespace marker. Package.swift declares swift-tools-version 5.9, platform macOS 14.

14.title: Create Swift test target stubs
14.summary: Adds test targets to Package.swift and creates corresponding test source directories under Crafted/Tests/. Each module gets a test directory with a single XCTestCase subclass containing one passing test (testPlaceholder). Targets: AppShellTests, AuthKitTests, KeychainKitTests, XPCBridgeTests, ProcessManagerTests, BuildStreamTests, SettingsTests, DocImportTests.

15.title: Add end-to-end scaffold validation tests
15.summary: Adds dedicated test files validating the entire repository scaffold: every src/ subpackage is importable, pyproject.toml is valid TOML with correct project name and version matching VERSION file, requirements.txt contains all required packages, conftest.py exists, forge-standards documents exist, Swift Package.swift is present and lists all expected targets, and every src/ subpackage has a corresponding tests/ subpackage.
